per_turn_leaks.student_message: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain started off as just a dull ache, but over time it's gotten sharper and crampier. It's like a stabbing sensation that comes in waves. And with the blood in my stool, I've also had an increase in bowel movements - I'm going 8-10 times a day now, which is way more than usual for me. The stools are loose and watery too, and I feel this urgent need to have a bowel movement all the time. It's been really uncomfortable and embarrassing, especially with the blood showing up in my stool.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've been feeling pretty queasy at times, especially after some bowel movements. It's like there's this knot in my stomach that doesn't go away for a while. But it's not just nausea - it's more of an overall gut feeling of being unwell. The crampy pain and bloody stools have really taken a toll on me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I haven't really noticed any significant changes in my appetite. If anything, I've probably eaten a bit more frequently due to the constant need to have bowel movements. As for weight loss, I haven't really been paying attention to it, but my girlfriend has mentioned that I seem thinner lately. However, I'm not sure if it's just muscle mass from losing strength or actual weight loss. (G06)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad has type 2 diabetes, but my mom is healthy. As for siblings, my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at a pretty young age, I think around her early thirties. (G20, G19)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad has type 2 diabetes, and it's something we keep an eye on, but my mom is healthy. My older sister was actually diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around her early thirties. That's about it for any serious family medical issues that I know of. (G20, G19)
